title: Lepidagathis diffusa
taxonomy: Lepidagathis diffusa is classified within the family Acanthaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the acanthus or thistle family. This family is part of the order Lamiales, a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The order Lamiales is further grouped into the clade asterids, which is a large clade of flowering plants that also includes the orders Gentianales and Boraginales. Lepidagathis diffusa is native to tropical regions and is a type of shrub or herb. The classification of Lepidagathis diffusa is an example of a complex and hierarchical system used to group plants into increasingly broader categories.

intro: Lepidagathis diffusa is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae, native to tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Brazil, where it is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which may aid in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Lepidagathis diffusa include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Lepidagathis diffusa is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, or dried and made into a powder for oral consumption, to take advantage of its health benefits.
